The 6-Li and 7-Li beams, with energies between
11 and 26 MeV and intensities of 5-10 partcle
The beam was not stopped in the Pb target
backing.  Consequently, the beam current, measured on
a Faraday cup after the target, was integrated in order
to obtain the charge accumulated at each energy.  For
the sao paulo measurements, the beam was stopped in the
target backing and the beam current was integrated on
the isolated and biased target holder.
-By authors.  Most of the fusion yield is concentrated
in just a few dominant evaporation residue channels.
However, there are some ambiguities in the assignments
to these channels.  Transitions leading to 63Zn and
65Zn and to (3N) and (P2N) for 66Zn] are identified by
the same two gamma-rays (669 KeV and 962 KeV) in 63Cu,
populated either directly or following 63Zn beta-decay.
Thus, in both reactions, these yields are presented by
their sum.  The statistical model predicts comparable
63Zn and 63Cu yields for 6Li+59Co, but predicts an
appreciable yield only for 63Cu in the 7Li+59Co
reaction.  A similar situation occurs for 64Cu and 60Co
the 6Li induced reactions and to (pn) and (alpha+pn)
for  7Li] since a 278-KeV gamma ray is present in both
